text: Medical Image Segmentation is a crucial step in image processing. A key aspect of any research in this area is the comparison of images to assess the accuracy of the resulting segmentation. Several challenges have been reported in the literature such as: choosing the appropriate evaluation metric, using different definitions for the same metric and the fact that most of the current implementations are not efficient, which causes a bottleneck when dealing with large volumes of data. Also, current evaluation metrics are designed to deal with the classical, non-fuzzy case of image segmentation.
